headline: 6 core protocol contracts and 1 governance token catalogued from GitHub; Ethereum mainnet only

findings.text: Official deployments documented in GitHub README for mainnet at https://github.com/capfina/protocol with 6 protocol contracts and governance token
CAP governance token address 0x43044f861ec040DB59A7e324c40507adDb673142 confirmed on Etherscan; all other addresses verified against official GitHub documentation
Audit links pinned but unable to complete fetch due to tool call limits; recorded as unknowns for next run
Protocol.chains specifies Ethereum only; single-chain protocol

rationale.verdict: Catalogued 7 addresses (1 token + 6 protocol contracts) from fetched GitHub README with verified chain (Ethereum mainnet). Governance token supply fixed at 120,000 CAP. Unable to verify audit reports due to fetch limitations.

evidence.shows: Official Cap Protocol repository README containing complete mainnet deployment table with 6 core contract addresses (Governance, Treasury, Products, Trading, Queue, Oracle)
CAP token confirmed as governance token with fixed supply and pausable functionality; contract uses Solidity 0.6.6

unknowns: D2: protocol.website (cap.app) returned network error; could not verify deployed_contracts_doc URL
D3: protocol.audit_links (https://github.com/cap-labs-dev/cap-audits) fetch failed due to tool call limits; audit scope addresses not verified
D4: GitHub deployment artifacts (broadcast/ or deployments/ folders) not inspected due to tool call exhaustion; deployment script addresses unverified
D6: No factory pattern contracts identified in README; child pool enumeration not applicable

protocol_metadata.about: Cap is a synthetic trading protocol enabling traders to submit orders priced by an oracle network. The protocol uses a treasury to manage trader losses and profits, with CAP (120,000 fixed supply) as the governance token for voting on system upgrades and treasury allocation.
